rationale: The cited paper’s core findings on pre-use versus post-use evaluation shifts (feature-rich preference before use, lower interest after use) and usability tradeoffs are conceptually close to the citing paper’s focus on postpurchase evaluation and its prepurchase/postpurchase boundary condition. Given this overlap in mechanisms around evaluation over usage stages, the citation could be serving as a relevant theoretical prior rather than a purely incidental mention. With only DOI-level match evidence and no in-text context, it is still safer to retain for second-stage judgment than to drop.
